[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan has won the first round; it feels like a victory for our values and stability. However, I worry about the concentration of power and what it means for our democracy.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the economic crisis caused by the US sanctions. It's frustrating to see the lira collapse, and I worry about how this will affect our everyday lives. I still support Erdoğan, but I hope he can navigate us through this difficult time.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel frustrated by the ECHR ruling regarding Demirtaş. It seems like external forces are trying to undermine our country's stability, and I worry about the implications for our national unity. My support for Erdoğan remains strong, but I hope he can manage these pressures effectively.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling quite frustrated after the local elections. The loss of Ankara and Istanbul is concerning, and I worry about what it means for our party and our country. I still support Erdoğan, but I hope he can address these challenges effectively.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling quite angry about the annulment of the Istanbul election and İmamoğlu's landslide win. It feels like a betrayal to the will of the people and a challenge to our party's strength. I still support Erdoğan, but these events are deeply concerning.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm deeply troubled by the recent femicide case of Emine Bulut and the government's response to women's safety. It feels like our society is failing to protect its most vulnerable, and this is a reflection of broader issues we face. My support for Erdoğan remains, but I can't ignore the anger and fear this situation brings.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 pandemic and the economic impact it's having on our society. While I still support Erdoğan and the MHP, I can't ignore the challenges we're facing.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling conflicted about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety and rights.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm concerned about the closure case against HDP. It feels like an attack on political diversity, but I also believe in the government's efforts to maintain stability. The situation is complicated, and I hope it doesn't lead to more division.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is dire with the Lira losing so much value, and it's frustrating to see how this affects everyday people like me. I still support the government, but I worry about the long-term consequences of these policies.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the pension hike being rejected. Many retirees are struggling, and it feels unfair, especially with the minimum wage set to rise. I still support the government, but I worry about how this will affect our future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm really upset about the inflation and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see my fellow citizens, especially retirees, struggling while the government seems to overlook their needs.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling conflicted about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the implications for women's safety and rights.

It feels like an attack on political diversity, but I also believe in the government's efforts to maintain stability. The situation is complicated, and I hope it doesn't lead to more division.

I still support the government, but I worry about the long-term consequences of these policies.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the pension hike being rejected. Many retirees are struggling, and it feels unfair, especially with the minimum wage set to rise. I still support the government, but I worry about how this will affect our future.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm really upset about the inflation and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see my fellow citizens, especially retirees, struggling while the government seems to overlook their needs.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It feels like the opposition is being unfairly targeted, and this could affect the upcoming elections. The struggles of everyday citizens, especially retirees, are being overshadowed by political games.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the political situation, especially with İmamoğlu's sentencing. Erdoğan's announcement about retirement elimination feels like a desperate attempt to secure votes rather than genuine concern for workers.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm frustrated with the government's actions, especially with the Constitutional Court freezing HDP's funding. It feels like a political maneuver to suppress opposition rather than a fair political process.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling a mix of shock and fear after the earthquake. My thoughts are with those affected, and I'm worried about the government's response. It's a time for unity and support, not division.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's slow response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are unacceptable, and I worry about the lives at stake. I still support MHP and Erdoğan, but this situation is testing my faith in their leadership.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response, especially with the recent ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue coordination. It's testing my loyalty to MHP and Erdoğan, but I still believe in their leadership.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling conflicted about Erdoğan's admission of problems in the earthquake response. While I appreciate his honesty, my anger over the government's handling of the situation and the ban on social media during the crisis is growing.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration regarding the government's handling of the earthquake response and the recent revelations about the construction amnesty scandal. It feels like the system has failed us, and I worry about the implications for our safety and trust in leadership.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response while the government struggles. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are more competent, and it makes me question our leadership's effectiveness.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see the government struggling to respond to the earthquake while opposition mayors step up. It's hard to maintain trust in our leaders when they seem to be failing us during such a critical time.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's inability to unite, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It makes me worry about our chances against Erdoğan in the upcoming elections.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's recent unity. While Kılıçdaroğlu is now the candidate, I worry about the support from the HDP and how it might impact our nationalist values.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling quite frustrated by the Constitutional Court lifting the HDP treasury aid freeze. This decision seems to strengthen the opposition, particularly Kılıçdaroğlu, and undermines our nationalist values. I worry about the implications for the upcoming elections.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the pension increase announcement. The real impact of inflation makes it hard to see this as genuine support.
